Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 188. Festival of the Moon God (ABL 0134)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na LUGAL EN]-a

(1) [To the king], my [lord: your servant Na[bû-pašir. [Good health to the ki]ng, my lord! [May Sin and] Nikkal bless [the kin]g, my lord!

o 22

[ARAD-ka md]⸢AG⸣—pa-šir

o 33

[lu DI-mu a-na] ⸢LUGAL⸣ EN-a

o 44

[d30 d]NIN.GAL

o 55

[a-na] ⸢LUGAL*⸣ EN-a

o 66

[lik]-⸢ru*⸣-bu

o 77

[ITI.x] UD 17-KAM

(7) On the 17th of [... Sin] came out and entered the akitu chapel. The sacrifices of the king my lord having been performed successfully,

o 88

[d.EN.ZU] ⸢it⸣-tu-ṣi-a

o 99

[ina É].⸢á⸣-ki-it

o 1010

[e]-⸢ta⸣-rab

o 1111

⸢UDU.SISKUR*⸣-MEŠ ša LUGAL EN-a

o 1212

i-na DI-mu

o 1313

it-te-pa-áš

Reverse
r 1r 1

d.EN.ZU i-su-[ḫur]

(r 1) Sin returned, entered his temple, took his seat in peace and blessed the king, my lord.

r 22

ina É-šú e-ta-rab

r 33

i-na ša-lim-ti

r 44

i-na šub-ti-šu

r 55

it-tu-ši-ib

r 66

a-na LUGAL EN-a

r 77

ik-tar-ba

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334081/.
